title: Risk Management System ‚Äî Hazard Log Update SLA (Art. 9/Annex IV)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'EU AI Act' ‚Üí 'Art.13'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=ai_risk sourcetype="rms:hazard" earliest=-90d
| eval opened=if(status="new",_time,null())
| eval reviewed=if(status="reviewed",_time,null())
| stats min(opened) as t_open, max(reviewed) as t_rev by hazard_id
| eval review_hours=round((t_rev-t_open)/3600,2)
| where isnull(t_rev) OR review_hours>168
| sort - review_hours
description: Measures time from new_hazard to hazard_reviewed in risk register stream for RMS operational effectiveness.
value: Measures time from new_hazard to hazard_reviewed in risk register stream for RMS operational effectiveness.
implementation: (1) Ensure hazard_id stable; (2) escalate unreviewed >7d; (3) link to incident index; (4) quarterly RMS committee export.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_risk` `sourcetype=rms:hazard` ‚Äî hazard_id, status, transition.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Ensure hazard_id stable; (2) escalate unreviewed >7d; (3) link to incident index; (4) quarterly RMS committee export.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_risk sourcetype="rms:hazard" earliest=-90d
| eval opened=if(status="new",_time,null())
| eval reviewed=if(status="reviewed",_time,null())
| stats min(opened) as t_open, max(reviewed) as t_rev by hazard_id
| eval review_hours=round((t_rev-t_open)/3600,2)
| where isnull(t_rev) OR review_hours>168
| sort - review_hours
```

Understanding this SPL

**Risk Management System ‚Äî Hazard Log Update SLA (Art. 9/Annex IV) (EU AI Act)** ‚Äî Measures time from new_hazard to hazard_reviewed in risk register stream for RMS operational effectiveness.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_risk` `sourcetype=rms:hazard` ‚Äî hazard_id, status, transition. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_risk; **sourcetype**: rms:hazard. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ai_risk, sourcetype="rms:hazard", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **opened** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **reviewed** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by hazard_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **review_hours** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnull(t_rev) OR review_hours>168` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Histogram (review_hours), Table (open hazards), Single value (mean review_hours)
visualization: Histogram (review_hours), Table (open hazards), Single value (mean review_hours)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
